docs.title: Euro-Office Documentation
docs.text:
Your sovereign office

Euro-Office is a self-hostable document server — documents, spreadsheets, presentations, and PDFs — designed to integrate with a platform such as Nextcloud.

It is not a standalone application; it requires a compatible storage backend to provide the user interface and file storage.

  • Quickstart

    Get Euro-Office running and connected to your platform in minutes.

    Install with Docker

  • Install

    Docker, Docker Compose, Debian/Ubuntu, and RHEL/Fedora installation paths.

    Installation guide

  • Develop

    Build Euro-Office from source, run the dev environment, contribute.

    Developer setup

docs.title: What's inside
docs.text:
• Introduction — what Euro-Office is and how its pieces fit together.
  • Installation — production deployment via Docker, packages, and native installs.
  • Integration — connecting Euro-Office to Nextcloud and other DMS platforms.
  • Development — building Euro-Office from source.

docs.title: Building from source
docs.text: Euro-Office builds entirely inside Docker via docker buildx bake, so the only host requirements are Docker + buildx and Git.

docs.title: Build the Docker image
docs.text:
cd DocumentServer/build
docker buildx bake

This produces the same image published as ghcr.io/euro-office/documentserver.

docs.title: Pin a version
docs.text:
PRODUCT_VERSION=8.1.0 docker buildx bake

PRODUCT_VERSION is stamped into the image and surfaced via /healthcheck.

docs.title: Build distribution packages
docs.text:
docker buildx bake packages

Produces .deb and .rpm artifacts under out/. See packaging plan for the layout these packages target.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
The full prerequisite checklist (Docker user-without-root, buildx container driver, Git SSH keys) is documented in DocumentServer/build/BUILD_REQUISITES.md. That file is the current source of truth.

Coming soon

Migration of the BUILD_REQUISITES content into this page, plus documentation for the cluster and develop bake groups.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Euro-Office accepts contributions through pull requests against the relevant component repository. The canonical guide currently lives in DocumentServer/CONTRIBUTING.md; the points below are a summary.

docs.title: Before you start
docs.text:
• For non-trivial changes, open an issue first to align on approach.
  • Read AGENTS.md if you are using AI-assisted tools — there are specific commit-trailer requirements.

docs.title: Code style
docs.text:
• 120-character line width.
  • 4-space indentation.
  • Commits are signed (DCO) and follow Conventional Commits v1.0.0.

docs.title: Pull request requirements
docs.text:
• Tests for new behavior (Jest, Mocha, or Chai depending on the subpackage).
  • Documentation updates for user-visible changes.
  • Two approvals before merge.

Coming soon

The full content of CONTRIBUTING.md will be migrated into this page, plus contributor onboarding for first-time contributors.

docs.title: Developer setup
docs.text: The full Euro-Office stack runs locally via the docker-compose-based environment in DocumentServer/develop/. It builds each component from source, exposes hot-reload paths for the editor UI, and includes a paired Nextcloud instance for end-to-end testing.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Docker 24+ with buildx.
  • 16 GB RAM minimum (the first build is heavy).
  • Git with SSH access to https://github.com/Euro-Office.
  • A Linux x86_64 or arm64 host. macOS works for everything except native-core C++ debugging.

docs.title: Clone and bootstrap
docs.text:
git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/Euro-Office/DocumentServer.git
cd DocumentServer/develop
./scripts/up.sh        # placeholder — exact entrypoint TBD

docs.title: Layout of the dev environment
docs.text:
The compose file spins up:

  • Euro-Office document server (built from local source).
  • Nextcloud (with the Euro-Office integration app pre-installed).
  • PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ.
  • An nginx front-end with self-signed TLS.

docs.title: Mobile / non-localhost testing
docs.text:
When testing from a phone or a separate machine, override the public URL via the docker-compose .env so the editor connects through your LAN IP.

Coming soon

The full reference for the develop environment, migrated from DocumentServer/develop/README.md. That document is currently the source of truth.

docs.title: Docker
docs.text: The recommended way to run Euro-Office in production is via the official Docker image, mounted with persistent volumes and fronted by a reverse proxy that terminates TLS.

docs.title: Pull and run
docs.text:
docker run -d --name eurooffice \
    --restart=always \
    -p 443:443 \
    -v eurooffice_data:/var/www/eurooffice/Data \
    -v eurooffice_log:/var/log/eurooffice \
    -e JWT_ENABLED=true \
    -e JWT_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32) \
    ghcr.io/euro-office/documentserver

Key flags:

  • JWT_ENABLED=true — refuse any unsigned API request. Always enable in production.
  • JWT_SECRET — the shared secret used by your DMS to sign requests. Store it somewhere durable.
  • -v …_data:/var/www/eurooffice/Data — persists document data across container restarts.
  • -v …_log:/var/log/eurooffice — keeps logs outside the container.

docs.title: Image tags
docs.text: Tag Use latest Most recent release. Pin in production. X.Y.Z Specific version. develop Bleeding-edge builds from main. Not for production.

docs.title: TLS
docs.text: Terminate TLS in front of the container. An upcoming TLS and reverse proxy guide will provide nginx and Caddy examples.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
The container accepts configuration via environment variables (to be covered per subsystem in an upcoming configuration section) or by mounting a local.json into /etc/eurooffice/documentserver/local.json.

Coming soon

This page will be expanded with the full environment-variable matrix migrated from DocumentServer/build/README.md.

docs.title: Source
docs.text: The Docker image is built from DocumentServer/build/ in the DocumentServer repository. To build it yourself, see building from source.

docs.title: Nextcloud
docs.text: Euro-Office has first-class Nextcloud integration via a dedicated Nextcloud app that connects to a running Euro-Office document server.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• A running Euro-Office document server reachable from your Nextcloud instance — see installation.
  • The JWT secret you configured in your document server, which you'll need to enter in the Nextcloud app settings.
  • Nextcloud 34 or later

docs.title: Install the Nextcloud app
docs.text:
1. In Nextcloud, go to Apps → Office & text → Euro-Office integration.
  2. Click Download and enable.

docs.title: Configure the app
docs.text:
1. In Nextcloud, go to Settings → Administration → Euro-Office.
  2. Enter the URL of your Euro-Office document server, including the protocol and port (e.g. https://eurooffice.example.com).
  3. Enter the JWT secret you configured in your document server.
  4. Click Save.

docs.title: Source
docs.text: The Nextcloud integration app lives at https://github.com/Euro-Office/eurooffice-nextcloud.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Euro-Office is a microservice-style platform. The diagram below shows the high-level component layout for a typical single-node deployment.

flowchart LR
    Browser[Web browser]
    Desktop[Desktop editor]
    Browser <--> DocService
    Desktop <--> DocService
    DocService <--> Redis[(Redis)]
    DocService <--> RabbitMQ[(RabbitMQ)]
    DocService <--> DB[(SQL database)]
    DocService --> Converter[FileConverter]
    Converter --> Core[Core C++ engine]
    DocService --> Storage[(Object storage)]
    Converter --> Storage
    AdminPanel <--> DB
    DocService --> Metrics[(StatsD)]

docs.title: Backend services
docs.text:
Service Role Default port DocService Real-time collaboration, Socket.io transport, document session orchestration. 8080 FileConverter Wraps native C++ converters for format translation. — AdminPanel License management and system monitoring UI. 9000 Metrics StatsD-based metrics emission. —

All four are Node.js processes living in the server repository.

docs.title: External dependencies
docs.text:
• Redis — Session state and real-time coordination.
  • RabbitMQ — Inter-service messaging.
  • SQL database — One of MySQL, PostgreSQL, MSSQL, or Oracle.
  • Object storage (optional) — S3-compatible or Azure Blob; falls back to local filesystem.

A configuration section covering the setup of each will follow.

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text: Editors are vanilla JavaScript with RequireJS modules — no React/Vue/Angular — built per document type and served as static assets. See components → web-apps.

docs.title: Native core
docs.text: A C++ rendering and conversion engine. Output artifacts are consumed by FileConverter at runtime. See components → core.

docs.title: Scaling out
docs.text: For multi-node deployments DocService, FileConverter, and storage scale horizontally behind a load balancer; Redis and RabbitMQ are clustered, and the SQL database is replicated. An upcoming high-availability configuration page will cover the topology in depth.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
Euro-Office is composed of several independent repositories. Each is built and versioned separately; the top-level DocumentServer repository ties them together via git submodules.

Repository Purpose server Node.js backend: DocService, FileConverter, AdminPanel, Metrics. web-apps Browser-based editors for documents, spreadsheets, presentations, PDFs, Visio. sdkjs JavaScript SDK implementing the Office Open XML document models. core C++ rendering and conversion engine. core-fonts Font assets bundled with the platform. DesktopEditors Native multi-format office suite for Windows, macOS, Linux. docker-base Base Docker image with build dependencies. docker-ci CI/build automation layers on top of docker-base. document-server-package M4-template-based DEB/RPM packaging. DocumentServer Top-level repository; aggregates the above as submodules. qa-acceptance Acceptance test suite for the platform.

docs.title: How to navigate the source
docs.text:
If you are looking for…

  • Backend code (Node.js) → server/
  • Editor UI → web-apps/
  • Document model logic → sdkjs/
  • Format converters / renderers (C++) → core/
  • Build & Docker image definitions → DocumentServer/build/
  • Local development environment → DocumentServer/develop/
  • Packaging (DEB/RPM) → document-server-package/

docs.title: Suggested reading order
docs.text:
If you are new to Euro-Office, read in order:

  1. Architecture — how the components fit together at runtime.
  2. Installation — production deployment paths in depth.

docs.title: Licensing
docs.text:
Euro-Office is licensed under the AGPL-3.0, inherited from its upstream ONLYOFFICE Document Server lineage.

For the full source license, see the LICENSE file in the DocumentServer repository.

Coming soon

This page will cover:

  • Commercial licensing options for Euro-Office.
  • The contributor license agreement (CLA) and signed-commit requirements.
  • Third-party component attributions and their licenses (FreeType, AGG, libxml2, Closure Compiler, etc.).
  • The binary-files audit that confirms source access for every shipped artifact.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Euro-Office is a self-hostable office suite combining a real-time collaborative document server, web-based editors for documents, spreadsheets, presentations, and PDFs, and desktop editors for Windows, macOS, and Linux.

It is a fork of ONLYOFFICE Document Server, maintained independently to ship on a release schedule and packaging story that fits sovereign-cloud deployments.

docs.title: What you get
docs.text:
• Document server — real-time collaboration, format conversion, REST API.
  • Web editors — Word/Excel/PowerPoint-compatible editors that run in any browser.
  • Desktop editors — native applications for offline editing.
  • DMS-friendly — Nextcloud integration is first-class; the REST API also supports custom DMS integrations.

docs.title: What this documentation covers
docs.text:
• Installation across Docker, package managers, and native binaries.
  • Configuration of databases, message queues, storage backends, and TLS.
  • Integration with Nextcloud and other document-management systems.
  • Operating a Euro-Office deployment: monitoring, backups, upgrades.
  • Building Euro-Office from source and contributing changes.

docs.title: What this documentation does not cover
docs.text:
• Code-level API reference
  • End user documentation

docs.title: Where to go next
docs.text:
• New to Euro-Office? Start with the architecture overview.
  • Ready to run something? Jump to the Docker installation.
  • Building from source? See the developer setup.
